segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: OK, Jack.
segments.zh: 好的，杰克。在我们规划关于制冷的展示之前，先讨论一下目前发现的内容吧。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Before we plan our presentation about refrigeration, let's discuss what we've discovered so far.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Fine, Annie.
segments.zh: 好的，安妮。不过我得承认我还没做多少研究。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Though I have to admit I haven't done much research yet.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Nor me.
segments.zh: 我也是。但我找到一篇关于冰屋的有趣文章。我在英国见过一些18和19世纪的冰屋，所以知道它们通常建在阴凉处或地下，靠近冬天可能结冰的湖泊。然后冰块可以被切割并储存在冰屋里。但我之前不知道用稻草或锯末隔热能让它们几个月不融化。古罗马人也有制冷技术。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: But I found an interesting article about icehouses.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I'd seen some 18th- and 19th-century ones here in the UK, so I knew they were often built in a shady area or underground, close to lakes that might freeze in the winter.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Then blocks of ice could be cut and stored in the icehouse.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: But I didn't realise that insulating the blocks with straw or sawdust meant they didn't melt for months.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: The ancient Romans had refrigeration, too.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I didn't know that.
segments.zh: 这我还真不知道。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Yes, pits were dug in the ground, and snow was imported from the mountains – even though they were at quite a distance.
segments.zh: 是的，地上挖坑，从山上运雪——尽管距离相当远。雪储存在坑里，底部结冰。冰和雪都拿来卖。冰比雪贵，我猜只有社会上的富人才买得起。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: The snow was stored in the pits.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Ice formed at the bottom of it.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Both the ice and the snow were then sold.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: The ice cost more than the snow and my guess is that only the wealthy members of society could afford it.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I wouldn't be surprised.
segments.zh: 我不意外。我还看到一篇关于现代家用冰箱的文章。用了好几种不同的技术，但对我来说太复杂了，理解不了。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I also came across an article about modern domestic fridges.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Several different technologies are used, but they were too complex for me to understand.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: You have to wonder what happens when people get rid of old ones.
segments.zh: 你不好奇人们处理旧冰箱时会发生什么吗？

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: You mean because the gases in them are harmful for the environment?
segments.zh: 你是说因为里面的气体对环境有害？

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。至少现在有很多机构可以安全回收大部分零件，但有些人还是会把旧冰箱扔到乡下。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: At least there are now plenty of organisations that will recycle most of the components safely, but of course some people just dump old fridges in the countryside.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: It's hard to see how they can be stopped unfortunately.
segments.zh: 不幸的是，很难阻止他们。在英国，我们每年总共要处理掉三百万台冰箱！

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: In the UK we get rid of three million a year altogether!

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: That sounds a lot, especially because fridges hardly ever break down.
segments.zh: 听起来很多，尤其是冰箱几乎从不坏。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。在这个国家，家用冰箱平均使用11年，很多能用20年甚至更久。所以如果把成本除以使用年限，冰箱和其他家用电器相比并不贵。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: In this country we keep domestic fridges for 11 years on average, and a lot last for 20 or more.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: So if you divide the cost by the number of years you can use a fridge, they're not expensive, compared with some household appliances.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: True.
segments.zh: 确实。我想制造商通过设计不同的颜色和款式来鼓励人们多花钱。我敢肯定我父母买第一台冰箱时几乎没什么选择！

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I suppose manufacturers encourage people to spend more by making them different colours and designs.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I'm sure when my parents bought their first fridge they had hardly any choice!

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Yes, there's been quite a change.
segments.zh: 是的，变化很大。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Right, let's make a list of topics to cover in our presentation, and decide who's going to do more research on them.
segments.zh: 好了，我们来列一下演示中要涵盖的话题，并决定谁来做进一步研究。之后我们再聚在一起规划下一步。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Then later, we can get together and plan the next step.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。先从制冷的好处以及如今被冷藏的商品范围开始怎么样？因为当然不仅仅是食物和饮料。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: How about starting with how useful refrigeration is, and the range of goods that are refrigerated nowadays?

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Because of course it's not just food and drinks.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: No, I suppose flowers and medicines are refrigerated, too.
segments.zh: 不，我想鲜花和药品也是冷藏运输的。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: And computers.
segments.zh: 还有电脑。我可以做这个，除非你特别想做。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I could do that, unless you particularly want to.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: No, that's fine by me.
segments.zh: 不，我无所谓。那冷藏对人们健康的影响呢？

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: What about the effects of refrigeration on people's health?

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I'm not terribly keen, to be honest.
segments.zh: 说实话，我不太感兴趣。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Nor me.
segments.zh: 我也是。一读到关于化学的东西，我的脑子就一片空白。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: My mind just goes blank when I read anything about chemicals.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Oh, all right then.
segments.zh: 哦，那好吧。我帮你个忙。但你欠我个人情，杰克。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I'll do you a favour.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: But you owe me, Jack.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那对食品生产者的影响呢？比如贫穷国家的农民能把产品出口到发达国家？也许这个适合你？

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: What about the effects on food producers, like farmers in poorer countries being able to export their produce to developed countries?

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Something for you, maybe?

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I don't mind.
segments.zh: 我不介意。应该挺有意思的。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: It should be quite interesting.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I think we should also look at how refrigeration has helped whole cities – like Las Vegas, which couldn't exist without refrigeration because it's in the middle of a desert.
segments.zh: 我觉得我们还应该看看制冷技术如何帮助了整座城市——比如拉斯维加斯，没有制冷的话它根本不可能存在，因为它位于沙漠中央。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。我在图书馆快速翻了一本经济学书，里面有一章讲的就是这类事情。如果你愿意做这部分，我可以把书名告诉你。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I had a quick look at an economics book in the library that's got a chapter about this sort of thing.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I could give you the title, if you want to do this section.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Not particularly, to be honest.
segments.zh: 说实话，不太想。一般来说，我觉得经济学书读起来很费劲。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I find economics books pretty heavy going, as a rule.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: OK, leave it to me, then.
segments.zh: 好吧，那交给我吧。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Thanks.
segments.zh: 谢谢。然后是运输，以及冷藏卡车带来的改变。我倒是不介意试试那个。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Then there's transport, and the difference that refrigerated trucks have made.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I wouldn't mind having a go at that.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Don't forget trains, too.
segments.zh: 别忘了火车。我读到过，早在19世纪40年代，美国就用冷藏火车车厢运输牛奶和黄油。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: I read something about milk and butter being transported in refrigerated railroad cars in the USA, right back in the 1840s.

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: I hadn't thought of trains.
segments.zh: 我没想到火车。谢谢。

segments.speaker: ANNIE
segments.en: Thanks.

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: Shall we have a separate section on domestic fridges?
segments.zh: 我们要不要单独列一个关于家用冰箱的部分？毕竟，这是每个人都熟悉的东西。

segments.speaker: JACK
segments.en: After all, they're something everyone's familiar with.

items.prompt: What did Annie discover from reading about icehouses?

pairs.note: discover 和 didn't realise 都表示'发现、意识到'，但 didn't realise 是否定形式，强调之前不知道。

pairs.q: insulating the blocks with straw or sawdust
pairs.p: insulating the blocks with straw or sawdust
pairs.note: 原文中完全相同的短语，直接对应题干中的关键信息。

pairs.q: meant they didn't melt for months
pairs.p: meant they didn't melt for months
pairs.note: 原文中完全相同的短语，说明绝缘的效果。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问 Annie 从阅读冰屋中发现了什么，定位句中有 'But I didn't realise that...' 引出新发现，即绝缘材料使冰块数月不融化。

items.prompt: What point does Annie make about refrigeration in ancient Rome?

pairs.p: snow was imported from the mountains
pairs.note: 题干用'refrigeration'概括，原文具体描述用雪和冰冷藏的方法。

pairs.p: my guess is that only the wealthy members of society could afford it
pairs.note: 题干问'point'，原文用'my guess'引出观点，指出只有富人买得起。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到雪从远处运来储存，冰和雪都出售，且冰更贵，推测只有富人买得起，对应题干关于古罗马冷藏的观点。

items.prompt: In connection with modern refrigerators, both Annie and Jack are worried about
What do Jack and Annie agree regarding domestic fridges?

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位。
That's right 表示同意，与 agree 同义。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的 domestic fridges 直接定位到原文，That's right 表明 Jack 和 Annie 同意。

pairs.note: 原文直接重复了refrigerated，帮助定位

pairs.p: the range of goods that are refrigerated
pairs.note: 题干省略了'range of'，但核心意思相同

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'goods that are refrigerated'与原文'the range of goods that are refrigerated'几乎一致，通过'goods'和'refrigerated'即可定位到该句。

pairs.p: effects of refrigeration on people's health
pairs.note: 题干是'effects on health'，原文是'effects of refrigeration on people's health'，加了具体对象'refrigeration'和所有格'people's'，但核心意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'effects on health'，原文中'effects of refrigeration on people's health'直接对应，通过识别'refrigeration'和'people's'可定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干问对食品生产者的影响，原文说‘帮你一个忙’，是间接表达影响的方式。

paraphrase.explain: 通过‘do you a favour’这个短语，暗示了说话者将采取行动，从而对食品生产者产生影响，帮助定位到答案B。

pairs.note: 题干说'对城市的影响'，原文说'帮助了整个城市'，意思相近，都是讲城市受到的影响。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'对城市的影响'，原文说'帮助了整个城市'，用'helped whole cities'替换了'impact on cities'，直接定位到答案。

pairs.note: 题干中的'transport'在原文中被具体化为'trucks'，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'refrigerated transport'在原文中对应'refrigerated trucks'，通过同义替换定位到答案所在句。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'domestic fridges'在原文中逐字出现，因此可以直接定位到该句。
